characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The good-natured butclueless fatherof Max. He's the next door neighbor of the Pete family, and is often brought into Pete's schemes as a sidekick. He ends up with Sylvia inAn Extremely Goofy Movie. Accidental Athlete: InAn Extremely Goofy Moviewhen he interferes with Max's practice and is even offered a spot with the Gammas by complete accident. Adaptation Relationship Overhaul: With Pete. In many continuities before and after this one, Goofy and Pete's dynamic is entirely antagonistic (and occasionally murderous). In theGoof Troopuniverse, the two of them are rivals and frenemies who team up to solve a problem about as often as they're pit against each other. Even inAn Extremely Goofy Movie, which is set seven years after the main series, the pair are still shown to hang out in their spare time. Adaptational Angst Upgrade: While Goofy has been a father to a son before this series (in his solo shorts from the 1950's), all the stress, fears, mistakes and heartbreak that comes with being a parent causes this incarnation of him a lot more emotional distress than it ever did before, especially inA Goofy MovieandAn Extremely Goofy Movie, where the fights between Goofy and Max are played for drama. Aesop Amnesia: Similar to Max, Goofy has learned a few times now that he should be a bit less doting and a bit less intrusive when it comes to his son's personal life, but it's a lesson that never quite manages to stick.

characters.name: Goofy Goof
characters.description: The good-natured butclueless fatherof Max. He's the next door neighbor of the Pete family, and is often brought into Pete's schemes as a sidekick. He ends up with Sylvia inAn Extremely Goofy Movie. Accidental Athlete: InAn Extremely Goofy Moviewhen he interferes with Max's practice and is even offered a spot with the Gammas by complete accident. Adaptation Relationship Overhaul: With Pete. In many continuities before and after this one, Goofy and Pete's dynamic is entirely antagonistic (and occasionally murderous). In theGoof Troopuniverse, the two of them are rivals and frenemies who team up to solve a problem about as often as they're pit against each other. Even inAn Extremely Goofy Movie, which is set seven years after the main series, the pair are still shown to hang out in their spare time. Aesop Amnesia: Similar to Max, Goofy has learned a few times now that he should be a bit less doting and a bit less intrusive when it comes to his son's personal life, but it's a lesson that never quite manages to stick.

characters.name: Max Goof
characters.description: The relatively serious and ambitious son of Goofy. He is the next door neighbor of the Pete family, and is PJ's best friend. He often comes up with schemes and involves PJ. InA Goofy Movie, he befriends Bobby and gets together with Roxanne. A Boy and His X: In "Great Egg-Spectations", Max expresses a desire to own a pet of his own, and he gets very attached to a baby dinosaur he found in the wild. He names her Bubbles, feeds her, plays with her, protects her from harm, and is eventually very saddened when she returns to the wilderness with her mother. Acquired Situational Narcissism: In "Talent To The Max", Max temporarily gets his ego inflated by a manipulative, talking magic hat, that tries to convince him he's a great magician who doesn't need anyone else. Adorably Precocious Child: InMickey's Once Upon a Christmas, Max begins to doubt Santa Claus’ existence and starts making some accurate descriptions on the logistics of his job when trying to look into it. Aesop Amnesia: Several episodes of the show — starting with "Meanwhile, Back at the Ramp" — revolve around Max being embarassed by Goofy and not wanting to grow up like him, only to remember by the end how much he actually loves and values his father. This same embarassment likewise serves as the emotional center of Max's arc inA Goofy Movie, only for him to go right back to being embarassed and annoyed by Goofy again in the sequel.

characters.name: Peter Pete Sr.
characters.description: The sneaky and self-centered husband of Peg and father of PJ and Pistol. He is the next door neighbor of the Goofs, and often brings Goofy along on his schemes. Abusive Parents: He can range from being a relatively distant but still concernedBumbling Dadto a harshly verbally abusive slave-driver with his son. With his daughter, he can range from spoiling her with love (due toParental Favoritism) to being somewhat neglectful. Adaptation Relationship Overhaul: With Goofy. In many continuities before and after this one, Goofy and Pete's dynamic is entirely antagonistic (and occasionally murderous). In theGoof Troopuniverse, the two of them are rivals and frenemies who team up to solve a problem about as often as they're pit against each other. Even inAn Extremely Goofy Movie, which is set seven years after the main series, the pair are still shown to hang out in their spare time. Adaptational Heroism: Compared to his typical portrayals, Pete comes across as a far nicer guy in this series. Notespeciallynice, as he's still aJerkass, but he's not an outright villain. Aesop Amnesia: Those who've watched ahead will know that his "I'm a terrible parent" breakdown in "Axed By Addition" (one of the earlier episodes) has no effect whatsoever on how he treats PJ the rest of the series — or even the rest of the episode, once he found out PJ wasn't really dying. He more or less learns the lesson again in "From Air to Eternity", and then the movies came around.

characters.name: Peter "PJ" Pete Jr.
characters.description: The anxious, soft-spoken and long-suffering son of Pete and Peg and brother of Pistol. He is the next door neighbor of the Goof family and Max's best friend. He is often dragged into Max's schemes as a sidekick. InA Goofy Movie, he befriends Bobby. InAn Extremely Goofy Movie, he gets together with Beret Girl. Acrofatic: Very overweight, but decent at skateboarding, biking, sledding, and dancing, able to run nearly as fast as Max, and able to make risky jumps when the situation calls for it. Affectionate Nickname: A fun variant. PJ is already only known by his initials, but Max likes to combine them and call him 'Peej' as a symbol of their friendship. Alliterative Name: His first and last name begin with "P". Ancestral Name: Is called "PJ", or Peter Junior, since his dad's name is also Peter.

characters.name: Peg Pete
characters.description: The wise and influential wife of Pete and mother of PJ and Pistol. She is the next door neighbor to the Goof family. She isn't present in the movies. Action Mom: To an extent, but she is capable of punching out a guy twice her size. According to Pete in "Peg Of The Jungle", she lifts weights at the gym and has a black belt in karate. Alliterative Name: Named after a deliberately alliterative incarnation of Pete. Almighty Mom: Can tell off her misbehaving children, her husband, and outside threats as simply as glaring and yelling a line. Ambiguous Situation: Peg and her daughter Pistol never appear inA Goofy MovieorAn Extremely Goofy Movie, not even when PJ disembarks for college in the latter film, due to the filmmmakers being unable to come up with a role for them. Because Peg is never acknowledged once in either movie, it's anyone's guess whether she's simply doing her own thing offscreen, or if she and Pete split up offscreen during theTime Skipbetween the series and the movies.noteGiven Pete's claims inAn Extremely Goofy Moviethat he will have the house all to himself once PJ goes to college, the implication that they broke up becomes more blatant.

characters.name: Pistol Pete
characters.description: The bratty and hyperactive daughter of Pete and Peg and sister of PJ. She is the next door neighbor to the Goof family. She does not appear in the movies. Ace Pilot: In "Hot Air", although everyone in the cast thinks she's too young. Action Girl: She has her moments, especially in herlimelight episodes"Hot Air" (in which she becomes anAce Pilot) and "Three Ring Bind" (in which she breaks animals out of a circus). Adorably Precocious Child: Despite being considerably younger, she has an intelligence on par with Max and PJ; it's just that she's usually much more wild and uncontrollable. Alliterative Name: Like Peg, she was named after a deliberately alliterative incarnation of Pete.

characters.name: Waffles and Chainsaw
characters.description: Furry Confusion: They are ordinary, non-anthropomorphic pets owned by anthropomorphic cats and dogs. Irony: Waffles the cat lives with anthropomorphic dogs, while Chainsaw is the dog of an anthropomorphic cat family. A Day in the Limelight: "Cat's Entertainment" is centered around Waffles starring in a cat food commercial. Ambiguous Gender: In some episodes, Waffles is male, and in others, Waffles is female. The female references happen early in the series, so it may have been aRetcon. Big Eater: It's very common to see Goofy or Max making or eating dinner and Waffles sitting by and mooching.

characters.name: Danielle Wrathmaker
characters.description: Prim and Proper Bun: Ties her hair up into a little roll and does not take her job as a news reporter lightly.

characters.name: Robert "Bobby" Zimmeruski
characters.description: Max and PJ's airheaded and rambunctious new friend in the movies. InA Goofy Movie, he gets together with Stacey. Actor Allusion: Of course, inExtremely Goofy, the writers simplyhadto have him say, "Where ya been, buuuuuuuuuuuudee?" Advertised Extra: In the first movie he had a minor role, but got significantly more screen time in the trailer than Pete and Roxanne (and PJ, who didn't appearat all). Ascended Extra: He had a minor albeit funny role inA Goofy Movie. Come the sequel he's promoted to a main character. Attention Whore: A variant on this. While he doesn'ttryto soak up attention, he clearly enjoys getting it and he can be a bit of a showoff. However, this is mainly due to being high on energy and low on self-restraint as opposed to craving the adoration of others. In fact, he seems to act larger than life irrespective of others. Notice his attitude while sitting in the principal's office. He's almost completely oblivious to Max and PJ's obvious terror.

characters.name: Roxanne
characters.description: Max's sweet, shy, and cute love interest and Stacey's best friend inA Goofy Movie. Not present inAn Extremely Goofy Movie. Birds of a Feather: Like Max, she is fairly awkward and requires help from her friends to get together with him. Breakout Character: Despite being aSatellite Love Interest, she is a very popular among the films' fanbase. Many 90s and 2000s kids even consider her an early fictional crush. Boyfriend-Blocking Dad: Her father is a bull of a man (dog) who is none-too-pleased to see Max whenever he stops by to see his daughter. He doesn't even formally talk to Max; he justglaresandgrowls. The Bus Came Back: She vanished in the second film, but she makes a special guest appearance inHouse of Mouse, appears in a few French comic strips, and has a blink-and-you-'ll-miss it cameo on one of Goofy's photos inDuckTales.

characters.name: Stacey
characters.description: Roxanne's popular and chatty best friend inA Goofy Movie. Gets together with Bobby at the end. Not present inAn Extremely Goofy Movie. Death Glare: She gives one at the nerd who's trying to hit on her. Inconsistent Spelling: The movie credits list her name as "Stacey", but fans spell it as "Stacy" just as often, and sometimes "Staci," even when writing reference material. Like Is, Like, a Comma: A side-effect of herMotor Mouthtendencies. Motor Mouth: She tends to speak quickly and for somewhat long periods of time. In one early conversation with Roxanne, she says a very long run-on sentence at an incredibly rapid rate (even compared to her other dialogue) before she finally notices that Roxanne isn't listening. Possibly anActor Allusionto her voice actress, Jenna von Oy, having a similar chatty best friend role asSixLeMureon NBC'sBlossomnotewhich incidentally was winding down production around the time the film was in production and was also co-produced by Disney subsidiaryTouchstone Pictures

characters.name: Powerline
characters.description: A popular singer that appears inA Goofy Movie. Actor Allusion: Tevin Campbell was a protégé ofPrince, so it's no surprise Powerline takes a few cues from the Purple One in terms of appearance and personality. Chest Insignia: As part of his electricity motif, his costume sports a stylized atom symbol dead-center on his chest. Consummate Professional: During the opening act of his concert, he has to deal with multiple equipment failures, a breakdown of security, and two random strangers crashing his show. Does he throw a fit and storm off? Nope. The audience loves it, so heincorporates them into the showlike nothing happened, and seems to actually have fun doing it. Cool Shades: In posters and standees, he wears a pair of rimless shades. He was initially going to wear these in the concerts in the storyboards, but he didn't.

characters.name: Principal Mazur
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: He gets thrown down a trap door during the mock Powerline concert, gets his toupee knocked off, and generally disrespected by his students. In fact, humiliating Mazur just makes Max more popular. Dean Bitterman: He's not exactly fond of students going on summer vacation, andthe students have no respect for him. The moment Principal Mazur walks onstage, the previously cheering crowd instantly goes dead silent, complete withChirping Crickets. He also believes that Max's antics deserve some seriousDisproportionate Retribution(even if he is unable to hand it himself), if the call he gives Goofy (and begins the whole plot) in which hehollers"control your kid before he ends up in the electric chair!" is any clue. Dodgy Toupee: Max accidentally knocks his hair loose during "After Today", revealing he's actually wearing one of these. It falls off again when he's dropped through a hatch in the floor by Bobby so Max can take the stage for the "Stand Out" number. Hair-Trigger Temper: Oh boy, does he sure have a nasty temper! Large Ham: His call to Goofy has Mazur exaggerate what Max did, complete with yelling at the top of his lungs with exaggerated facial expressions. Even during the assembly where he suggests very boring things in summer vacation, he'smaking wild movements with his arms and getting huge grins on his face.

characters.name: Bigfoot
characters.description: Bigfoot, Sasquatch, and Yeti: Goofy manages to get Bigfoot on video through sheer dumb luck, but Bigfoot destroys the tape when he rummages through their stuff, so any evidence of him is destroyed along with it. Contemporary Caveman: His attitude is that of a neanderthal who has some very primitive speech capabilities. He uses two socks to put on a "puppet show" to tease Max and Goofy, which neither of them are particularly amused by. He's also smart enough to know that Goofy and Max won't be able to drive away without their car keys and purposefully throws them away. It's also shown that he at least somewhat understands modern language, as rummaging through Goofy's stuff has him find a tape player with headphones that starts playing "Stayin' Alive" byThe Bee Gees, which makes Bigfoot make a grunt that sounds like "Music!" as the song starts. Bigfoot then starts dancing to the song, recreating the "John Travoltapose" from the poster ofSaturday Night Feveras he does. Enemy Eats Your Lunch: Played with. Goofy's "perfect cast" demonstration hooks a steak from Pete's grill, which flies onto land. Bigfoot grabs the steak to eat it, but he gets pulled to Goofy along with the steak, prompting Bigfoot to attack in anger. Funny Background Event: Messing around with Goofy and Max's things nets him a pair of headphones that play "Stayin' Alive" byThe Bee Gees, which makes Bigfoot say "Music!" as the song starts. Bigfoot then starts dancing to the song, recreating the "John Travoltapose" from the poster ofSaturday Night Feveras he does behind the car as Goofy and Max heat up a can of alphabet soup. Loud Sleeper Gag: Both Bigfoot and Goofy snore so loudly that Max can't get any sleep. With both Goofy and Bigfoot snoring, the camera cuts to Max, eyes bloodshot red and wide awake.

characters.name: Bradley Uppercrust III
characters.description: A snobby and vicious team leader. He's the main antagonistof An Extremely Goofy Movie. Arrogant Kung-Fu Guy: He does have a posse, although he'snot very loyal to them.He still wants to put the "losers" in their place. Bad Boss: He treats his teammates like crap, and ultimately sees them as expendable, as shown with what he ends up doing to Tank during the X-games finale. Big Bad: OfAn Extremely Goofy Movie, as hisEvil Planinvolves Bradley making a bet with Max's team during the X Games so that whoever loses the games is forced to be the other team's towel boy. Big Jerk on Campus: The head of the most elite fraternity on campus is also a bully and cheater who commits multiple felonies.

characters.name: Beret Girl
characters.description: PJ's snarky and artsy girlfriend inAn Extremely Goofy Movie. Badass Pacifist: When the Gammas are bullying PJ and Bobby, she gets everyone's attention by shining a spotlight, loudly and publicly trash-talks Bradley without hesitation, and then manages to get everyone in the coffeehouse to rhythmically snap until he and his team express annoyance and leave. She never physically hurts anyone. Beatnik: Introduced reciting slam poetry in the coffee house, wears black sweaters and a beret, has bongos in her arrangement, and uses slang such as "daddy-o" and Buddhist jargon. Birds of a Feather: She and PJ are both interested in beatnik poetry, have a fairly snarky attitude, and care more about personality than appearance. Cats Are Snarkers: She's got a poetic and dry sense of humor.

characters.name: Sylvia Marpole
characters.description: Goofy's passionate and good-humored girlfriend inAn Extremely Goofy Movie. Birds of a Feather: She and Goofy are bothDisco Dantypes. Closet Geek: She tries to retain a professional attitude, but when Goofy starts talking to her she switches intoFangirlmode. Covered in Kisses: She does this to Goofy after spotting the bouquet of flowers he brought for her. Disco Dan: What made Goofy fall for her primarily is her passion for '70s memorabilia.

characters.name: Tank
characters.description: Bradley's loud and burly right-hand man inAn Extremely Goofy Movie. Affably Evil: Despite being the brute of the Gammas, he's much more pleasant and courteous than his boss. Boisterous Bruiser: Notably big enough to dwarf and pick upPJ, as well as being loud, confident, and tough. The Dog Bites Back:After Bradley leaves him to die and Max's team wins the X-Games, Tank slingshots Bradley into the X-Games blimp in revenge. The Dragon: He is shown to be the Gammas' right hand man, and, other than Bradley, is the only one who's fleshed-out at all.However, he eventually has aHeel–Face Turnwhen Bradley ditches him.
